## 4. Structural Diagrams & Schematics

### 4.1 Block Diagram — Series Hybrid Drive-Train Power Flow

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[Fuel Tank] --> B[ICE Engine]
    B --> C[Generator]
    C --> D[Rectifier / DC Bus]
    E[Battery Pack] -->|charge discharge| D
    D --> F[Traction Inverter]
    F --> G[Traction Motor]
    G --> H[Final Drive]
    H --> I[Wheels]
    J[Auxiliary Loads] -.-> D
```

### 4.2 Block Diagram — Parallel Hybrid Drive-Train Power Flow

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A1[Fuel Tank] --> B1[ICE Engine]
    C1[Battery Pack] --> D1[Motor Inverter]
    D1 --> E1[Traction Motor]
    B1 --> F1[Clutch C1]
    E1 --> F1
    F1 --> G1[Transmission]
    G1 --> H1[Final Drive]
    H1 --> I1[Wheels]
```

### 4.3 Block Diagram — Series-Parallel (Power-Split) Drive-Train

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A2[Fuel Tank] --> B2[ICE Engine]
    B2 --> C2[Planetary Carrier]
    D2[Battery] --> E2[Inverter 1]
    E2 --> F2[MG1 Sun Gear]
    D2 --> G2[Inverter 2]
    G2 --> H2[MG2 Ring Gear]
    C2 --> I2[Planetary Gearset]
    F2 --> I2
    I2 --> J2[Ring Output]
    J2 --> K2[Final Drive]
    H2 --> K2
    K2 --> L2[Wheels]
```

### 4.4 Functional Architecture — EMS Hierarchy

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    M1[Driver Pedal Input] --> M2[Demand Interpretation]
    M2 --> M3[Energy Management Strategy EMS]
    M3 --> M4{Topology Type}
    M4 -->|Series| M5[Engine Setpoint + Battery Schedule]
    M4 -->|Parallel| M6[Torque Split + Clutch Logic]
    M4 -->|Series-Parallel| M7[Planetary Speed Control + MG1 MG2 Split]
    M5 --> M8[Component Actuators]
    M6 --> M8
    M7 --> M8
    M8 --> M9[Throttle Clutch Inverters]
```

### 4.5 Power-Flow Comparison Matrix (Tabular Form)

| Operating Mode | Series Path | Parallel Path | Power-Split Path |
|----------------|-------------|----------------|-------------------|
| Idle / Stop | ICE off, battery → motor | Both off | Engine off, MG2 drives |
| Light Load | ICE charges battery | Electric only | Engine on, MG1 generator |
| Cruising | ICE → gen → motor | ICE only or both | Engine → wheels direct |
| Hard Acceleration | ICE + battery → motor | ICE + motor | Engine + MG2 to wheels |
| Braking | Motor regen → battery | Motor regen → battery | MG2 regen + MG1 spin |

## 5. KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

### Part A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

#### Question 1 [KTU University Exam – May 2024]
**"Define a Series Hybrid Electric Vehicle. Draw its power flow path and state one advantage and one disadvantage."**

**Model Answer:**
A *Series Hybrid Electric Vehicle* is one in which the Internal Combustion Engine (ICE) is mechanically decoupled from the wheels. The engine drives a generator; the generated electrical energy either charges the battery or is inverted to drive a traction motor connected to the wheels.

**Power Flow Path:** ICE → Generator → Rectifier → DC Bus ↔ Battery ↔ Inverter → Traction Motor → Wheels.

- **Advantage:** ICE can operate continuously at its BSFC-optimum point → high city-cycle efficiency.
- **Disadvantage:** Two energy conversions (mechanical-electrical-mechanical) cause ~15 % cumulative losses.

*(Examiner marks: 1 mark definition + 1 mark path + 1 mark advantage/disadvantage.)*

#### Question 2 [KTU University Exam – Dec 2023]
**"What is the role of an Energy Management Strategy (EMS) in a hybrid vehicle?"**

**Model Answer:**
The EMS is the supervisory controller that determines, in real time, the optimal power split between the ICE and the electric machine(s) to:
1. Meet the driver's torque/power demand.
2. Maintain battery SOC within prescribed limits (typically 0.3–0.8).
3. Minimize equivalent fuel consumption and tail-pipe emissions.
4. Protect drivetrain components from overload and thermal damage.

Common EMS types include *Rule-based* (deterministic IF-THEN maps) and *Optimization-based* (ECMS, Dynamic Programming).

---

### Part B — Long Answer Questions (14 Marks Each)

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Pitfall Alert:** In power-flow derivations, students frequently:
> - Drop efficiency terms ($\eta_g$, $\eta_m$) → loses **1 mark**.
> - Confuse *charge* and *discharge* sign convention → loses **1 mark**.
> - Forget units in the final answer → loses **0.5 mark** per occurrence.
> - Skip the planetary-gearset derivation assumption (carrier on engine) → loses **2 marks**.

---

#### Question A (14 Marks) [KTU University Exam – May 2024]

**(a)** Derive the power-balance equation for a Series Hybrid Electric Vehicle, clearly showing the role of the generator efficiency $\eta_g$, traction-motor efficiency $\eta_m$, and auxiliary load $P_{aux}$. **(7 Marks)**

**(b)** A series hybrid city bus has the following data at an instant:

- Engine brake power $P_{eng} = 80\ \mathrm{kW}$
- Generator efficiency $\eta_g = 0.92$
- Battery charging power $P_{ch} = 12\ \mathrm{kW}$
- Traction motor efficiency $\eta_m = 0.90$
- Auxiliary load $P_{aux} = 4\ \mathrm{kW}$

Calculate the power delivered to the wheels. **(7 Marks)**

**Model Solution:**

**(a) Derivation** — Recapitulate §3.1, Steps 1–5. Use the combined equation:

$$
P_{wheel} = \eta_m(\eta_g P_{eng} - P_{ch}) - P_{aux}
$$

Mention explicitly that during charging, $P_{ch}$ is positive and subtracted from bus power before inversion.

**(b) Numerical substitution:**

$$
P_{wheel} = 0.90\bigl(0.92 \times 80 - 12\bigr) - 4
$$

$$
P_{wheel} = 0.90\bigl(73.6 - 12\bigr) - 4
$$

$$
P_{wheel} = 0.90 \times 61.6 - 4
$$

$$
P_{wheel} = 55.44 - 4
$$

$$
\boxed{P_{wheel} = 51.44\ \mathrm{kW}}
$$

> [!NOTE]
> **Answer Verification:** A reasonable traction motor of $\sim 75\ \mathrm{kW}$ capacity should not exceed 80 % loading → $75 \times 0.8 = 60\ \mathrm{kW}$. Our 51.44 kW is consistent. *(Sanity check worth 0.5 marks in valuation key.)*

---

#### Question B (14 Marks) [KTU University Exam – Dec 2023]

**(a)** Explain with a neat block diagram the operation of a Parallel Hybrid drive-train and derive the torque-coupling equation at the wheel. **(7 Marks)**

**(b)** In a parallel hybrid, the ICE delivers $T_{eng} = 120\ \mathrm{Nm}$ at $\omega_{eng} = 3000\ \mathrm{rpm}$, and the traction motor delivers $T_{mot} = 80\ \mathrm{Nm}$ at the same shaft speed. The transmission ratio is $i_g = 1.8$, final-drive ratio $i_f = 3.5$, and drivetrain efficiency $\eta_t = 0.93$. Compute the torque and power delivered to the wheels. **(7 Marks)**

**Model Solution:**

**(a) Explanation + Derivation:**
State that the parallel hybrid has ICE and traction motor on the *same* shaft (or via a torque coupler) so that both can drive the wheels directly. Reference the block diagram from §4.2 and derive:

$$
T_{wheel} = (T_{eng} + T_{mot})\,i_g\,i_f\,\eta_t
$$

$$
P_{wheel} = (P_{eng} + P_{mot})\,\eta_t
$$

**(b) Numerical substitution:**

$$
T_{wheel} = (120 + 80) \times 1.8 \times 3.5 \times 0.93
$$

$$
T_{wheel} = 200 \times 1.8 \times 3.5 \times 0.93
$$

$$
T_{wheel} = 200 \times 6.3 \times 0.93 = 1260 \times 0.93
$$

$$
\boxed{T_{wheel} = 1171.8\ \mathrm{Nm}}
$$

Now compute wheel speed:

$$
\omega_{wheel} = \omega_{eng}/(i_g i_f) = \frac{3000}{1.8 \times 3.5} = \frac{3000}{6.3}
$$

$$
\omega_{wheel} \approx 476.19\ \mathrm{rpm} \approx 49.87\ \mathrm{rad/s}
$$

Power at wheels:

$$
P_{wheel} = T_{wheel}\cdot \omega_{wheel} = 1171.8 \times 49.87
$$

$$
\boxed{P_{wheel} \approx 58.43\ \mathrm{kW}}
$$

---

### Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

- **Series HEV:** ICE decoupled; efficient in city; suffers double energy-conversion loss.
- **Parallel HEV:** ICE and motor share shaft; highest highway efficiency; needs clutch logic.
- **Series-Parallel (Power-Split):** Uses planetary gearset; combines both benefits; used in Toyota THS, Ford Hybrid.
- **EMS** decides the torque/power split every 10–100 ms based on SOC, demand, and efficiency maps.
- **ECMS equivalence factor $s$** converts battery power into "equivalent fuel" → enables real-time optimization.
- **SOC window 0.3–0.8** — outside this, EMS triggers charge-sustain or charge-depleting modes.
- **Planetary Willis equation** $\omega_s = (1+k)\omega_c - k\omega_r$ is the *kinematic heart* of the Toyota Prius-type hybrid.
- **Wheel-load equation** uses road-load coefficients: $F = f_r mg + \tfrac{1}{2}\rho_a C_d A v^2$.
- **Always carry units** through every derivation step — KTU examiners explicitly deduct for unitless final answers.
- **Valuation key standard:** A 14-mark question expects ~3–4 derivation steps plus 1 numerical; missing any one step costs 2 marks.
